quoted_tweet.text:
🇨🇳China Halts New Autonomous Vehicle Licenses After Baidu Robotaxi Incident
China has suspended issuing new licenses for autonomous vehicles after dozens of Baidu Apollo Go robotaxis stopped operating in Wuhan last month, stranding passengers and disrupting traffic, according to people familiar with the matter. Three regulators, including the Ministry of Industry and Information Technology, convened a meeting with local officials overseeing robotaxi pilots and called for safety reviews and enhanced monitoring. The suspension prevents companies from expanding fleets or launching new projects in additional cities. Baidu’s Wuhan robotaxi operations have also been suspended pending investigation. $BIDU 
(https://t.co/2FgORZoMnV)

tweet.text:
$BIDU

First of all, apologies for the long post.

Right now on the chart we can see $BIDU is currently testing the confluence of the 50-day and 200-day MAs at $120, if the price can hold above this level, then we can begin looking at the resistance that will come from the top of Wave 1 at $165.

Break above $165 would remove any concerns of a deeper pullback, and would confirm we are in Wave 3, with a target at the 1.618 Fib at $252.

The downside risk here, is if the 2 MAs fail to hold for support, then the 0.786 Fib at $94 would likely come into play.

Now I want to share my view on thefundamentals, because this is where things get very interesting.

$BIDU is currently trading with a market cap around $41B. At the same time, Waymo’s last private valuation came in around $126B.... Just for a comparison.

Let that sink in.

The market is effectively valuing ALL of Baidu, search, cloud, AI, Apollo autonomous driving, ERNIE models, and cash at roughly one third of Waymo alone.

Even more important, $BIDU has a very strong balance sheet...... With roughly $20B+ in net cash and investments, a huge portion of the current valuation is backed by cash.... Which is just insane!

This is where the asymmetric opportunity comes in -

Profitable core search business

Growing cloud segment

Strong AI positioning with ERNIE

Autonomous driving through Apollo

Large net cash position

When you break it down further -

Search: $5.5B

Cloud: $12.9B

Apollo: $2.9B

Kunlun AI Chip: $17B

Net cash: $19.3B

iQIYI: $761M

Total estimated value comes out around $170 per share vs where it trades today at $120ish...

The cash alone is massive...

Looking at the valuation metrics -

P/E: 80.65 (distorted by accounting/one-offs)

Forward P/E: 13.28

PEG: 1.14

P/S: 1.88

P/B: 1.08

EV/EBITDA: 13.44

This is not the profile of an overvalued company. If anything, it reinforces the idea that the market is heavily discounting future growth and optionality.

For me, this is what makes $BIDU so attractive at these levels...

You have a technically defined setup with clear invalidation and upside targets, combined with a fundamentally undervalued business that has multiple growth levers.

In my opinion, this is one of those positions where the numbers are hard to ignore or even impossible to ignore.

At some point, the market will start pricing this correctly.

And when it does, it won’t happen slowly.

And yes, I did see on the news about the 100 cars that stalled... For me this is a complete overreaction.
